summary.intent: Implement three GitHub issues (#297, #298, #299) adding 8 framework-agnostic color components (ColorSwatch, ColorField, ColorArea, ColorSlider, ColorWheel, AngleSlider, ColorSwatchPicker) plus a shared ColorValue foundation to the ars-ui Rust component library, then drive the PR through CI and Codex automated review to approval.
summary.outcome: All 8 components implemented with ~4100 tests, CI fully green (all 22 checks including coverage), and PR #698 created with auto-close keywords. 54 Codex findings addressed across 12 review rounds; the session ended mid-pass-12 (fix committed, push/reply/re-trigger pending at session close).

repo: ars-core is built no_std via --no-default-features in CI; transcendental float (powf, cbrt, atan2) requires the workspace's established `core_maths` crate with FQ form `CoreFloat::method(x)`. core_maths lacks min/max so private fmin/fmax helpers are needed.

The workspace denies `clippy::suspicious`; a pre-existing pagination lint (map_or_else → unwrap_or_else) became a CI blocker when stable toolchain bumped to 1.96. No pin — any new lint deny can surface pre-existing issues.

`spec validate` and `spec-compile-snippets` are separate gates; spec validate is NOT part of xci-fast, so spec frontmatter drift (e.g. multi-line `related:` array) can slip through the pre-push gate and fail only on direct validation.

Coverage CI uses `cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch --workspace`; the --workspace flag gives ~1.1% branch boost over -p alone because cross-crate integration tests exercise ars-components. Measuring coverage with -p undershoots CI.
Branch coverage requires both arms of every one-armed `if flag {}` conditional; connect methods with flag-gated attribute writes were the largest gap class.
`snapshot-count` xci-fast gate requires exactly 1 snapshot per declared state variant (not just 'some tests pass'); discovered when AngleSlider had 2 snapshots for 3 states.
CLAUDE.md mandates spec sync after any contract change; the spec agent pattern (dedicated subagent with exact change instructions) is the established workflow for this.
MessageFn has hand-written From<F> impls per closure signature arity; adding a new message type requires extending message_fn.rs, not just adding a type alias.

Bindable::get() returns the controlled/parent value while pending()/internal holds the staged value. Drag machines must use pending() for display/ARIA/hidden input to keep position and announcements consistent during controlled drags.

ColorValue::new wraps hue into [0,360), so hue 360 becomes 0. Drag sliders/wheels that expose the endpoint need a separate unwrapped `slider_value`/`hue_value` field as display source of truth; the stored ColorValue is still normalized.

SyncValue must preserve the cached tracked value (slider_value/hue_value/x_value/y_value) when the incoming parent color matches the current pending color to avoid dropping the 360° endpoint on a controlled echo.
Disabled guards must let DragEnd through so a mid-drag disable does not wedge the machine in Dragging state permanently (same for readonly).

The `parse_error` (parser-derived invalid) and `invalid` (externally supplied prop) must be tracked separately in ColorField; SetProps must only update the external flag so a prop refresh cannot clear a parser failure.
RTL horizontal controls need both render (position pct mirrored) and pointer inversion (1.0 - normalized x/position) to keep the physical start-drag gesture pointing at min.

parse_hsb_args and parse_hsl_args must clamp channel values before the HSB→HSL math; out-of-range inputs (e.g. 200%) produce wrong colors without clamping. trim_start_matches('#') strips all leading # — strip_prefix is correct for at-most-one.

code.finding: ColorValue::new only debug_asserts finiteness; in release builds NaN/inf components silently propagate. Added is_finite() coercion to 0.0 before wrap/clamp.
from_hex must use strip_prefix('#').unwrap_or(hex) — not trim_start_matches('#') which strips ALL leading # and also panics at non-char boundaries on non-ASCII input like #ああ. Added is_ascii() guard.

MessageFn only works with pre-declared From<F> impl arities. Added four color-specific signatures: Fn(&ColorNameParts,&Locale), Fn(ColorChannel,&Locale), Fn(ColorChannel,f64,&Locale), Fn(f64,f64,&str,&str,&Locale), then &str/&str/&Locale for slider/area axis readings.

Added slider_value: f64 to Context as the unwrapped display source for hue channel (0..=max); ColorValue stored remains normalized. IncrementX/SetToMax/DragMove all operate on slider_value; ARIA/CSS/hidden input read it instead of channel_value(color.get()).

Added parse_error: bool field separate from ctx.invalid. commit_input/adjust_channel/snap_channel set parse_error; SetInvalid/SetProps/init set ctx.invalid; SyncValue/SetValue clear both. Api::is_invalid() = invalid || parse_error guards all connect output.

background_attrs now exposes data-ars-x-channel/y-channel + four CSS corner-color custom properties (tl/tr/bl/br) so adapters can render the correct 2D gradient for any axis combination, with RTL x-flip and y-inversion baked in.

f64::clamp panics on min>max or NaN bound; replaced with private clamp_to_range(value, min, max) helper that guards non-finite and swapped bounds. Also const fn display_value() returns pending() for consistent controlled-mode ARIA.

Pre-existing map_or_else(None, identity) clippy::unnecessary_option_map_or_else lint surfaced on stable 1.96 and blocked CI because workspace denies clippy::suspicious. Fixed incidentally as part of this PR.

workflow: The spec-sync subagent pattern (SendMessage to a named agent with a structured change list) scales well for mechanical spec mirroring after implementation — much faster than doing it inline and protects the main context window.
TDD throughout: write the failing test first, run it to confirm red, then implement. This made every Codex finding's fix verifiable and prevented regressions.
Background watcher tasks (Bash run_in_background polling gh api) work well for Codex review lifecycle polling. Use a non-read-only variable name in zsh scripts (avoid `status`, `REPLY`, etc.).
Codex rate limits ('reached your Codex usage limits') block the review bot entirely — it's an account quota, not a code problem. Nothing to do but wait for reset.

12 rounds of Codex review is not unusual for a large PR with controlled-mode semantics; the findings per round trend downward (8→6→6→2→3→3→5→8→5→5→3→1→...) but controlled-mode and RTL corner cases generate many rounds.

cargo xci-fast (10 gates including snapshot-count, spec-compile-snippets, error-variant-coverage, mutual-exclusion) is the pre-push gate but does NOT include spec validate — run that separately after spec edits.
Measuring coverage accurately requires matching CI's exact invocation (--workspace, nightly, --branch); -p-only measurements undershoot by ~1% and can give false confidence.

friction: Codex rate-limited ('reached your Codex usage limits') blocked the first re-trigger; had to wait for quota reset before the first real review pass.
Coverage CI failed (89.7% < 90% branch) after the initial implementation — one-armed if conditionals in all 6 stateful machines needed both-arms tests added.
core_maths::CoreFloat lacks min/max; discovered mid-implementation after the dep was already added and code written. Required private fmin/fmax helpers and re-auditing all float call sites.
TransitionPlan closure type inference E0282 throughout — every `|ctx|` closure in every machine needed explicit `|ctx: &mut Context<M>|` annotation.
Spec files referenced multiple non-existent APIs (use_platform_effects, track_pointer_drag, generate_id, ColorSwatchMessages) requiring reconciliation before the first commit.
Pre-existing pagination lint blocker on stable 1.96 (clippy::suspicious denied workspace-wide) was unrelated to the PR but had to be fixed to unblock CI.
Codex re-raised already-fixed issues at least once (M3 in pass 8 was a stale re-raise of L3 fixed in pass 7); required recognizing and dismissing rather than re-implementing.
12 rounds of Codex review with 54 findings over many hours — the review loop ran much longer than the implementation itself.
Push race conditions between background tasks and foreground retries caused redundant 'nothing to do' push failures; needed to detect the remote-ahead case explicitly.
zsh `status` is a read-only variable; background watcher scripts crashed until renamed to `cov_state`.
Spec agent occasionally introduced markdown formatting changes (multi-line related: arrays, misaligned tables) that broke spec validate or triggered MD060 warnings in subsequent validation.
open_items: PR #698 merge awaiting user approval + Codex 👍 (pass 12 fix committed but push/reply/re-trigger was the last incomplete step at session close).
Issues #297/#298/#299 auto-close only on merge — not yet closed.
Adapter work (Leptos/Dioxus color-* components) explicitly out of scope for this PR; will need follow-up issues/tasks.
ColorValue could be promoted from color-picker.md §Types to spec/shared/color-types.md — noted by the spec agent as an orthogonal improvement not pursued.

spec/foundation/04-internationalization.md MessageFn From-impl listing is incomplete (omits the color-component signatures added in this PR) — the spec agent deliberately skipped it to avoid partial listing inconsistency.
Codex usage quota will need to reset before any future automated review passes on this or other PRs.
